stem: A decision-maker must choose among 3 energy source options. The probability of favorable regional climate data is uncertain, estimated between 39% and 56%. The payoffs (in $K) for each option are: solar: $196K in good regional climate data, $90K in bad regional climate data. wind: $189K in good regional climate data, $84K in bad regional climate data. natural gas: $240K in good regional climate data, $99K in bad regional climate data. Which option maximizes expected value? Does the optimal choice change across the range of probability estimates? What is the expected value of the best option?

ground_truth.answer: At P(good)=0.48: best option is natural gas with EV=$166.7K. natural gas is optimal across the entire uncertainty range. EV range of best option: [154.0, 178.0].

solution_steps: Compute EV for each option: EV = P(good)×payoff_good + P(bad)×payoff_bad.
natural gas is optimal across the entire uncertainty range.
common_failure_modes: Evaluating options only at the midpoint probability without sensitivity analysis
Not identifying the breakeven probability where the optimal choice switches
Ignoring downside risk and focusing only on expected value
